meta.name: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A reliable, universal classic chocolate chip cookie with crisp edges and a chewy center. Includes both standard all-purpose flour and fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one).

equipment.label: Parchment paper or silicone baking mat

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (Option A — use this OR the fresh-milled option)
ingredients.notes: About 3 cups if spooned/leveled, but weighing is best.

ingredients.label: Fresh-milled flour, fine-milled (Option B — use this OR the AP option)
ingredients.notes: Fresh-milled flour often absorbs more. If dough is dry/crumbly, add 1–3 tbsp (15–45 ml) milk or water during mixing.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: If using fine table salt, reduce to ~4 g.

ingredients.label: Chopped walnuts or pecans (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional mix-in; toast for more flavor.

ingredients.label: Milk or water (only if needed for fresh-milled flour dough)
ingredients.notes: Start with 15 ml (1 tbsp) and only add if dough is crumbly/dry; up to ~45 ml may be needed depending on flour.

steps.title: Prep pans and oven
steps.body: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line 2 baking sheets with parchment (or silicone mats).

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the all-purpose flour ingredient OR the fresh-milled flour ingredient (not both). If using fresh-milled flour, rest the dough at least 60 minutes for hydration and add a little milk/water if needed.

steps.title: Whisk dry ingredients
steps.body: In a medium bowl, whisk together your chosen flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.

steps.title: Cream butter and sugars
steps.body: In a large bowl, beat softened butter with brown sugar and granulated sugar until lightened and fluffy, 2–3 minutes. Scrape the bowl well.

steps.title: Add eggs and vanilla
steps.body: Add eggs one at a time, mixing until fully incorporated after each. Mix in vanilla. Scrape the bowl again.

steps.title: Add dry ingredients
steps.body: Add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and fold until just combined—no dry streaks. Do not overmix.

steps.title: Adjust hydration if using fresh-milled flour
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and the dough looks crumbly or too stiff, add milk or water 15 ml (1 tbsp) at a time until thick but scoopable (often 15–45 ml total). If dough becomes greasy/too soft, chill 15–30 minutes.

steps.title: Fold in chocolate (and nuts if using)
steps.body: Fold in chocolate chips (and nuts, if using) until evenly distributed.

steps.title: Rest the dough (baseline cookie logic)
steps.body: Cover and rest 30–60 minutes at room temperature, or refrigerate 1–24 hours for deeper flavor and better texture. Fresh-milled flour benefits from at least 60 minutes rest.

steps.title: Scoop and space
steps.body: Scoop dough into 2-tbsp portions (about 35–40 g each) and place 2–3 inches apart on prepared sheets. For thicker cookies, form tall mounds rather than balls.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake 10–12 minutes, rotating the pan halfway if needed. Pull cookies when edges are set and lightly golden, but centers still look slightly underdone—carryover heat finishes them.

steps.title: Cool to set texture
steps.body: Cool on the baking sheet 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool completely.

steps.title: Store or freeze
steps.body: Store airtight at room temperature 3–5 days. To freeze dough: scoop portions, freeze on a tray until firm, then bag airtight up to 2–3 months. Bake from frozen at 350°F (175°C), adding 1–2 minutes.

notes: Fresh-milled flour varies a lot. If your grind is coarse, consider sifting out the largest bran for a more classic cookie texture.
Texture control: more brown sugar increases chew; more granulated sugar increases crispness. This recipe balances both.
Doneness control: pull early for chewy centers; bake 1–2 minutes longer for crisper cookies.
